title: docker-compose sometimes causes podman.service to stop responding
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am using docker-compose to start a number of services on reboot. Occasionally the podman system service gets stuck and stops responding to any commands till I restart it. I am not sure if it is due to the number of compose files I am running concurrently or not. I have at times let the `docker ps` run for around 5 minutes.

Additionally an strace on the `podman system service` shows the following:
```
[pid  1815] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>,  <unfinished ...>
[pid  1496] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>,  <unfinished ...>
[pid  1815] <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 0, NULL, 6) = 0
[pid  1496] <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 0, NULL, 0) = 0
[pid  1815] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>,  <unfinished ...>
[pid  1496] futex(0xc000a94150, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL <unfinished ...>
[pid  1191] <... nanosleep resumed>NULL) = 0
[pid  1191] futex(0x55a4b6f5ed38, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, {tv_sec=0, tv_nsec=90822077} <unfinished ...>
[pid  1815] <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 99, NULL, 7) = 0
[pid  1815] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>, [], 128, 0, NULL, 0) = 0
[pid  1815] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>,  <unfinished ...>
[pid  1191] <... futex resumed>)        = -1 ETIMEDOUT (Connection timed out)
[pid  1191] nanosleep({tv_sec=0, tv_nsec=10000000},  <unfinished ...>
[pid  1815] <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 1, NULL, 7) = 0
[pid  1815] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>, [], 128, 0, NULL, 0) = 0
[pid  1815] futex(0xc000a94150, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
[pid  1496] <... futex resumed>)        = 0
[pid  1815] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>,  <unfinished ...>
[pid  1496] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>,  <unfinished ...>
[pid  1815] <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 0, NULL, 6) = 0
[pid  1496] <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 0, NULL, 0) = 0
[pid  1815] epoll_pwait(5<anon_inode:[eventpoll]>,  <unfinished ...>
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a systemd unit
```
# /etc/systemd/system/docker-compose@.service 
[Unit]
Description=%i service with docker compose
After=NetworkManager-wait-online.service srv.mount podman

[Service]
Type=oneshot
RemainAfterExit=true
WorkingDirectory=/etc/compose/
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/docker-compose -f /etc/compose/%i-compose.yml down 
ExecStart=/usr/bin/docker-compose -f /etc/compose/%i-compose.yml up -d --force-recreate
ExecStop=/usr/bin/docker-compose -f /etc/compose/%i-compose.yml down 

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

2. Create a few compose files
```
# /etc/compose/seafile-compose.yml 
version: "2"
services:
  seafile-db:
    image: mariadb:10.1
    container_name: seafile-mysql
    restart: always
    environment:
      - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=password  # Requested, set the root's password of MySQL service.
      - MYSQL_LOG_CONSOLE=true
    volumes:
      - /srv/seafile-mysql/db:/var/lib/mysql:Z  # Requested, specifies the path to MySQL data persistent store.
    networks:
      - seafile-net

  memcached:
    image: memcached:latest
    restart: always
    container_name: seafile-memcached
    entrypoint: ["memcached", "-m", "256" ]
    networks:
      - seafile-net
          
  seafile:
    image: seafileltd/seafile-mc:latest
    restart: always
    container_name: seafile
    ports:
      - "8000:80"
    volumes:
      - /srv/seafile-data:/shared:Z   # Requested, specifies the path to Seafile data persistent store.
    environment:
      - DB_HOST=seafile-db
      - DB_ROOT_PASSWD=password  # Requested, the value shuold be root's password of MySQL service.
      - SEAFILE_ADMIN_EMAIL=me@example.com # Specifies Seafile admin user, default is 'me@example.com'.
      - SEAFILE_ADMIN_PASSWORD=asecret     # Specifies Seafile admin password, default is 'asecret'.
      - SEAFILE_SERVER_LETSENCRYPT=false   # Whether to use https or not.
      - SEAFILE_SERVER_HOSTNAME=seafile.local # Specifies your host name if https is enabled.
    depends_on:
      - seafile-db
      - memcached
    networks:
      - seafile-net
networks:
  seafile-net:
```
```
# /etc/containers/prometheus-compose.yml
version: "2"
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
    restart: always
    hostname: prometheus
    container_name: prometheus
    volumes:
      - /var/lib/container-data/prometheus/etc:/etc/prometheus:Z
      - /var/lib/container-data/prometheus/data:/prometheus:Z
  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    restart: always
    hostname: grafana
    container_name: grafana
    volumes:
      - /var/lib/container-data/grafana/etc:/etc/grafana:Z
      - /var/lib/container-data/grafana/var:/var/lib/grafana:Z
    ports:
     - "9000:3000"
```
3. `systemctl enable docker-compose@prometheus docker-compose@seafile`
4. Reboot and some services will fail (it is not always the same one).
5. Observe that `podman ps` does not return and some services failed to startup with docker socket timeouts

```
Jul 05 16:44:44 docker-compose[1481]: Creating prometheus ...
Jul 05 16:45:45 docker-compose[1481]: ERROR: for prometheus  UnixHTTPConnectionPool(host='localhost', port=None): Read timed out. (read timeout=60)
Jul 05 16:45:45 docker-compose[1481]: ERROR: for grafana  UnixHTTPConnectionPool(host='localhost', port=None): Read timed out. (read timeout=60)
Jul 05 16:45:45 docker-compose[1481]: ERROR: for prometheus  UnixHTTPConnectionPool(host='localhost', port=None): Read timed out. (read timeout=60)
Jul 05 16:45:45 docker-compose[1481]: ERROR: for grafana  UnixHTTPConnectionPool(host='localhost', port=None): Read timed out. (read timeout=60)
Jul 05 16:45:45 docker-compose[1481]: An HTTP request took too long to complete. Retry with --verbose to obtain debug information.
Jul 05 16:45:45 docker-compose[1481]: If you encounter this issue regularly because of slow network conditions, consider setting COMPOSE_HTTP_TIMEOUT to a higher value (current value: 60).
```

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman ps` hangs till a `systemctl restart podman.service` is issued

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect for the podman.service/socket to continue accepting commands and not hang.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.2
API Version:  3.2.2
Go Version:   go1.16.4
Built:        Fri Jun 25 19:49:19 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  buildahVersion: 1.21.0

  security:

    rootless: false

    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json

  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:

  swapFree: 8589930496

    paused: 0

  graphDriverName: overlay

    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs

  imageStore:
    number: 17
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

  APIVersion: 3.2.2

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.2.2-1.fc34.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
N/A

comments.body: Do you see any errors in the journal? `journalctl -r -u podman.service` and  `journalctl -r -u podman.socket`

@kevinanderson1 Does `podman pod ps` hang as well? That command uses different set of locks. Depending on it's behavior then we can determine which lock is hanging you up. A thread dump of the podman service would also be helpful. /cc @mheon

I apologize for the delay in responding. Life got a little bit hectic. 

@Luap99 I do not see any errors for those services. The last few lines (sorted in reverse order) are below
```
Jul 23 16:25:58 guardian.lan.kevinanderson.dev podman[1212]: time="2021-07-23T16:25:58-04:00" level=info msg="APIHandler(954da3d2-e69d-4af3-9376-fa8fbb156af1) -- GET /v1.24/containers/json?limit=0 BEGIN"
```

@jwhonce `podman pod ps` does return successfully (although I do not have any pods defined which is expected). I was a little unsure of how to generate an appropriate thread dump so I tried just about every way I could think of and find. If there is an alternative way to gather a thread dump that you would prefer if you let me know I can do that the next time I encounter the problem.

`  Goroutine 67 - User: /usr/src/debug/podman-3.2.3-2.fc34.x86_64/_build/src/github.com/containers/podman/libpod/shutdown/handler.go:45 github.com/containers/podman/libpod/shutdown.Start.func1 (0x56034dbfa36b) [select 452566h37m36.224710718s`

This immediately jumps out to me. The shutdown handler is running, so Podman is trying to spin down and exit. It is possible to inhibit shutdown for a time so essential processes can complete, and my current bet is that is what is happening.

@mheon I did try to set the podman service to run indefinitely by appending `--time=0` to ensure it was not shutting down due to inactivity and I am still able to pretty reliably reproduce the service hang.
@jwhonce Was working on some leaks in the podman service, could these be related?

I upgraded to podman 3.3.0 this evening and so far all of my containers seem to start with docker-compose and podman returns successfully without having to restart the podman service. I plan to let it run for a few more days but this issue might be fixed. I'll try to repeatedly test it throughout the week and report back Monday on the results.
Ok reopen if the issue comes back.
